qa_pairs.question: What is the value of a GPU instance with a 1g profile?
qa_pairs.answer: A GPU instance with a 1g profile is worth 1/7 of an A100 or H100 GPU.

qa_pairs.question: What special consideration is given to a 3g GPU instance profile?
qa_pairs.answer: The 3g GPU instance profile takes into consideration the extra amount of memory per 'g'.

qa_pairs.question: Why are 4g GPU profiles not available on the clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: To simplify things for users, the 4g profiles are not available on the clusters.

qa_pairs.question: For applications primarily performing FP32 operations, how does an A100-40gb GPU compare to a P100-12gb GPU in terms of speed and recorded usage?
qa_pairs.answer: An A100-40gb GPU is expected to be twice as fast as a P100-12gb GPU for FP32 operations, but its recorded usage will be four times the resources.

qa_pairs.question: If running FP32 operations, which GPU allows for double the computations for an equal amount of RGUs: an A100-40gb or a P100-12gb?
qa_pairs.answer: For an equal amount of RGUs and applications primarily performing FP32 operations, P100-12gb GPUs should allow you to run double the computations compared to an A100-40gb GPU.

qa_pairs.question: For AI-related applications primarily doing FP16 operations, how much more resource usage is an A100-40gb evaluated as compared to a P100-12gb?
qa_pairs.answer: For AI-related applications primarily doing FP16 operations, an A100-40gb is evaluated as using 4 times the resources of a P100-12gb.

qa_pairs.question: For FP16 operations, how many more calculations can an A100-40gb GPU perform compared to a P100-12gb GPU in the same amount of time?
qa_pairs.answer: For FP16 operations, an A100-40gb GPU is capable of computing approximately 30 times more calculations than a P100-12gb GPU for the same amount of time.

qa_pairs.question: If using an A100-40gb GPU for FP16 operations, how many more computations can be completed compared to a P100-12gb GPU?
qa_pairs.answer: Using an A100-40gb GPU for FP16 operations would allow you to complete approximately 7.5 times more computations compared to a P100-12gb GPU.

qa_pairs.question: What must a proposal asking for GPUs specify during the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: During the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), any proposal asking for GPUs must specify the preferred GPU model for the project.

qa_pairs.question: How are Reference GPU Units (RGUs) calculated in the CCDB form for RAC proposals?
qa_pairs.answer: In the CCDB form, the amount of Reference GPU Units (RGUs) is automatically calculated from the requested amount of GPU-years per year of project.

qa_pairs.question: If 13 GPU-years of an A100-40gb model are requested for the narval-gpu resource, what is the corresponding amount of RGUs?
qa_pairs.answer: If 13 GPU-years of an A100-40gb model are requested for the narval-gpu resource, the corresponding amount of RGUs would be 52.

qa_pairs.question: What factor determines the final RGU allocation by the RAC committee?
qa_pairs.answer: The RAC committee allocates RGUs depending on the proposal score.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a GPU allocation needs to be moved to a different cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: If a GPU allocation must be moved to a different cluster, the committee will allocate GPU-years at that cluster so as to keep the amount of RGUs the same.

qa_pairs.question: What is the main principle for calculating job priority on national clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: The main principle for calculating job priority on national clusters is that compute-based jobs are considered based on the resources others are prevented from using, not on the resources actually used.

qa_pairs.question: When do unused cores contribute to a job's priority calculation?
qa_pairs.answer: Unused cores contribute to a job's priority calculation when a submitted job requests multiple cores but uses fewer than requested during execution.

qa_pairs.question: What core usage metric affects the priority of future jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: The number of cores requested, not the number of cores actually used by the application, affects the priority of future jobs.

qa_pairs.question: Why does the number of requested cores, not actual usage, affect future job priority?
qa_pairs.answer: The number of requested cores affects future job priority because any unused cores were unavailable to other users during the job's execution.

qa_pairs.question: What is another common scenario where resource usage affects priority calculation?
qa_pairs.answer: Another common scenario is when a job requests memory beyond what is associated with the cores requested.

qa_pairs.question: How is usage calculated if a job requests one core but 8GB of memory on a cluster with 4GB per core?
qa_pairs.answer: If a job requests one core and 8GB of memory on a cluster with 4GB per core, it will be deemed to have used two cores.

qa_pairs.question: Why is a job requesting 8GB of memory with a single core (on a 4GB/core system) deemed to have used two cores?
qa_pairs.answer: It is deemed to have used two cores because other researchers were effectively prevented from using the second core due to a lack of available memory.

qa_pairs.question: What is a core equivalent?
qa_pairs.answer: A core equivalent is a bundle consisting of a single core and a certain amount of associated memory.

qa_pairs.question: How else can a core equivalent be defined?
qa_pairs.answer: A core equivalent can also be defined as a core plus the amount of memory considered to be associated with each core on a given system.

qa_pairs.question: What is the typical definition of a core-equivalent on most clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: On most clusters, a core-equivalent is defined as 4GB of memory and a core, reflecting the most common node type's memory-to-core ratio.

qa_pairs.question: How are jobs charged in terms of core-equivalent usage?
qa_pairs.answer: Jobs are charged at a rate of 4 GB per core for core-equivalent usage.

qa_pairs.question: When is allocation target tracking straightforward for core usage?
qa_pairs.answer: Allocation target tracking is straightforward when resource requests consist entirely of core and memory amounts that can be portioned only into complete equivalent cores.

qa_pairs.question: What complicates allocation target tracking for core equivalents?
qa_pairs.answer: Allocation target tracking becomes more complicated when jobs request portions of a core equivalent, as many points can be counted against a research group's allocation even for partial usage.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the Alliance's method for accounting for system usage?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance's method for accounting for system usage aims to solve problems related to fairness and perceptions of fairness.

qa_pairs.question: How are research groups charged for core equivalent usage?
qa_pairs.answer: Research groups are charged for the maximum number of core equivalents they take from the resources.

qa_pairs.question: How are research groups charged if they use more cores than memory, relative to the 1 core/4GB ratio?
qa_pairs.answer: Research groups using more cores than memory, above the 1 core/4GB ratio, will be charged based on cores.

qa_pairs.question: If a research group requests two cores and 2GB per core (4GB total) on a system with a 1 core/4GB equivalent, how many core equivalents will be counted for priority calculation?
qa_pairs.answer: If a research group requests two cores and 2GB per core (4GB total) on a system with a 1 core/4GB equivalent, the job will be counted as 2 core equivalents for priority calculation.
